opportunities.title: Consensys Hired a North Korean Dev and Didn't Notice — Your KYC Process Won't Either
opportunities.why_now: A major, security-conscious Web3 firm just admitted it unknowingly onboarded a DPRK operative as a contractor, proving the IT-worker infiltration threat has moved upmarket from small startups to blue-chip infra.
opportunities.angle: Explainer/investigation: walk through how NK developer laundering works (fake resumes, VPN farms, referral chains) and what due-diligence signals Consensys missed — end with a checklist for hiring managers.

opportunities.title: Kaspersky Just Mapped the Malware Framework That's Been Draining Wallets Quietly All Year
opportunities.why_now: Kaspersky's new report names a specific, active framework targeting crypto investors right now — this is a rare case of attribution-grade research landing before the losses get big headlines.
opportunities.angle: Technical breakdown for the security-literate reader: infection vector, persistence mechanism, and how it differs from commodity stealers — framed as 'the thing that gets you before you even see the phishing link.'

opportunities.title: Your Mac Isn't Safe Either: New Malware Hijacks Telegram Sessions to Empty Wallets
opportunities.why_now: SlowMist just documented macOS-specific malware actively targeting crypto users via Telegram session hijacking, killing the 'Macs don't get malware' assumption that many holders still operate on.
opportunities.angle: Practical security post: how session-hijacking bypasses 2FA entirely, why Telegram-based deal flow is a bigger attack surface than people admit, plus concrete hardening steps.

opportunities.title: ZachXBT Says Hardware Wallets Are 'Complete Garbage' — Trezor's Rebuttal Proves His Point
opportunities.why_now: A live, public spat between a top on-chain investigator and a leading hardware wallet maker is happening right now, forcing an overdue reckoning on whether cold storage still protects against modern social-engineering attacks.
opportunities.angle: Opinion/analysis piece dissecting both sides: hardware wallets stop remote key theft but do nothing against blind-signing and drainer scams — reframe the debate as 'wrong threat model, not wrong product.'

opportunities.title: SummerFi Is Dead. The Exploit Was Just the Autopsy, Not the Cause of Death
opportunities.why_now: A seven-year-old DeFi protocol just announced it's shutting down citing an exploit — a clean, timely case study in how slow-bleed technical debt and thin margins turn one hack into a project-ending event.
opportunities.angle: Post-mortem format: reconstruct the exploit timeline, then argue the real story is treasury/runway exhaustion that made recovery impossible — a template for 'protocols that die from hacks vs. protocols that die because of them.'
